intro:
There is limited information on the plant × Elyhordeum dakotense. However, I can provide information on Elymus hystrix and Hordeum jubatum, which share similar characteristics with × Elyhordeum dakotense. 

Elymus hystrix is a type of grass, commonly known as bottlebrush grass due to its unique inflorescence shape, which is a characteristic of the genus Elymus. The health benefits of Elymus hystrix include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.

The bioactive constituents of Elymus hystrix include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenolic glycosides, which are responsible for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Elymus hystrix include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
